rulings.Case Title: FRANK MOOREFIELD et al VS. AW CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. Objections to interrogatories are not evidence. Decedent's testimony insufficient to shift the burden of proof. Weber vs. Crane (2006) 143 Cal.app. 4th 1433. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MOOREFIELD et al VS. AW CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied. Failure to shift burden of proof. To the extent interrogatory responses are unverified, they cannot support the motion. See Appleton vs. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636. Defendant ignores decedent's exposure to its products other than turbines and household appliances. (See Hastler depo pgs. 28-30; Fountain declaration. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MOOREFIELD et al VS. AW CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT QUINTEC INDUSTRIES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Verifications to plaintiffs' interrogatories responses are provided. Defendant obtained substantive responses to its special interrogatories that, combined with deposition testimony, is sufficient to shift the burden. Plaintiffs fail to create a triable issue of fact because plaintiffs offer no new evidence in opposition to this motion. Plaintiff's objections to evidence overruled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MOOREFIELD et al VS. AW CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT CRANE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary adjudication denied. Unverified discovery responses are tantamount to nothing. Appleton vs. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636. Failure to shift burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MOOREFIELD et al VS. AW CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof and plaintiffs fail to raise a triable issue of fact. Only products decedent allegedly exposed to were USG/Durabond and Bakelite. UCC did not market its Calidria asbestos for use in wallboards (Myers declaration) . USG/Durabond joint compound utilized asbestos from several suppliers(Bowman declaration.) Defendant did not make Bakelite plates that contained asbestos. (Martino declaration.) No triable issue of fact raised that decedent was exposed to defendant's products. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DALE PAYNE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT MOORE BROS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and discovery responses are not factually void. Plaintiff testified that he worked with defendant's asbestos blankets, gloves, and sleeves. Defendant does not present any evidence that it did not deliver supplies to plaintiff's job sites. Defendant admits it was a distributor of industrial and welding supplies, including asbestos blankets and asbestos gloves. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA FORSMANN VS. SUTTER HEALTH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN AFFILIATE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT HOSPICE BY THE BAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. (Matter will be heard at 10:30 am.) Motion for summary adjudication denied. Plaintiffs raise a triable issue of fact regarding the existence of a special relationship. The primary consideration is whether the special employer has the right to control and direct the activities of the alleged employee or the manner and method in which the work is performed. Caso vs. Nimrod Productions, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App. 4th 881, 888-889. Bilych testified she had to follow HBB's registered nurse's directions and was acting under the registered nurse's supervision. (Bilyck depo pgs. 60 and 63.) A triable issue of fact is raised regarding Clopton's knowledge of Bilyck's activities. (Clopton depo 102-103; 108-110). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY CALLAHAN VS. ROD GOYA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT ROD GOYA, and, CARMEL MISSION PARTNERSHIP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied. Triable issue of fact whether the statute of limitations should run from 2006 when plaintiff discovered from Dr. Marinkovich that her illness was due to mold. Triable issue of fact raised whether the 2006 lease containing the waiver provision (paragraph 8(c)) would bar any claim. Triable issue of fact regarding which lease would control over the various causes of action. Triable issue of fact raised whether plaintiff was commercially frustrated from using the unit she leased for the specific purpose of running her office. Triable issue of fact whether paragraph 6(b) of the 2006 lease would apply to bar the claim for commercial frustration in light of paragraphs 6(a), 7(b), and 7(c) which appear to not bar claims where defendant is at fault. =(302/CWW)
